zh.lifeSummary: 當Ermentrude von Morsberg於大約1109年出生在法國德 艾斯特莫瑟勒馬利蒙吉利斯-貝內斯特洛福時，她的父親Adalbert II. von Mörsberg45歲，母親Mathilde de Mousson30歲。她和Adalbert I von Kyburg Dillingen至少有6個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1180年3月12日享年72歲。

nameMeanings.name: Morsberg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Sven, Bjorn, Ludvig.  German Bernhard, Kurt.  Jewish Chaya, Herschel, Hyman.
Swedish: ornamental name composed of the elements mosse ‘peat bog’ + berg ‘mountain, hill’.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial extension of Moses , with German Berg ‘mountain, hill’.

variants.name: Mossburg
meanings: Possibly also an Americanized form of German Mosberg, see Mossberg .

variants.name: Moses
meanings: Jewish, Welsh, African American, and African (mainly Nigeria): from the Biblical personal name borne by the Israelite leader who led the Israelites out of Egypt, as related in the Book of Exodus. The Hebrew form of the name is Moshe . It is probably of Egyptian origin, from a short form of an ancient Egyptian personal name such as Rameses or Tutmosis, meaning ‘conceived (by a god)’. However, very early in its history the name acquired a folk etymology, being taken as a derivative of the Hebrew root verb mshh ‘extract or draw (something), e.g. from water’ and was associated with a story of the infant Moses being discovered among the bullrushes by Pharaoh's daughter (Exodus 2: 1-10). As a Welsh surname, it was adopted among Dissenter families in the 18th and 19th centuries. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Italian Moise , Hungarian Mózes (see Mozes ), Assyrian/Chaldean Moshe , Arabic Musa .
English: variant of Moss , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
English: variant of Moyses, a Cornish personal name derived from Middle English Moises, a vernacular form of Moses (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Ermentrude
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic origin, adopted from Old French by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It is derived from erm(en), irm(en) ‘entire’ + traut ‘beloved’. It did not survive long into the Middle Ages, but was occasionally revived in the 18th and 19th centuries. It is now completely out of fashion.

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Ronald .  It is sometimes used as a given name in its own right.

variants.name: Ronald
meanings: From the Old Norse personal name Rögnvaldr (composed of regin ‘advice, decision’ (also, ‘the gods’) + valdr ‘ruler’). This name was regularly used in the Middle Ages in northern England and Scotland, where Scandinavian influence was strong. It is now widespread throughout the English-speaking world.

en.lifeSummary: When Ermentrude von Morsberg was born about 1109, in Marimont-lès-Bénestroff, Moselle, Grand Est, France, her father, Adalbert II. von Mörsberg, was 45 and her mother, Mathilde de Mousson, was 30. She had at least 6 sons and 1 daughter with Adalbert I von Kyburg Dillingen. She died on 12 March 1180, at the age of 72.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ermentrude von Morsberg est née environ 1109, à Marimont-lès-Bénestroff, Moselle, Grand Est, France, son père, Adalbert II. von Mörsberg, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Mathilde de Mousson, avait 30 ans. Elle a eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille avec Adalbert I von Kyburg Dillingen. Elle est décédée le 12 mars 1180, à l’âge de 72 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ermentrude von Morsberg um 1109 in Marimont-lès-Bénestroff, Moselle, Großer Osten, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Adalbert II. von Mörsberg 45 und ihre Mutter Mathilde de Mousson 30. Sie hatte mit Adalbert I von Kyburg Dillingen mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 12. März 1180 im Alter von 72 Jahren.

ko.lifeSummary: Ermentrude von Morsberg(이)가 대략 1109년 프랑스 그랜드 에스트 모셀 마임트-e스-브e네스트로프에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Adalbert II. von Mörsberg(은)는 45세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mathilde de Mousson(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 Adalbert I von Kyburg Dillingen(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1180년 3월 12일 72세의 나이로 사망했다.
